title: Listening - Part 4: Learning Musical Instruments
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about learning to play a musical instrument as adults. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I started the cello at forty. [21]I’d always regretted giving up the piano as a child, and I felt I had some unfinished business with music[/21]. It was a bit of a mid-life challenge. What I’ve found most rewarding, though, is [26]the way it’s improved my ability to listen[/26]. Not just to music, but to everything. I’m much more aware of pitch, tone, and the rhythm of people’s voices now. It’s like a whole new sense has been switched on. || Speaker 2: I took up the drums because [22]my job as a surgeon is so precision-based and intense that I needed a physical way to vent my frustration at the end of the day[/22]. Hitting things with sticks is very therapeutic. Surprisingly, [27]it’s actually helped my physical coordination in the operating theatre[/27]. The independence you need between your hands and feet has made me much more dexterous in my professional work. || Speaker 3: I’m learning the guitar through YouTube tutorials. [23]I didn't want the pressure of a formal teacher or exams; I just wanted to be able to play a few songs for my kids at bedtime[/23]. It’s been great for that. But [28]what I really value is the mental escape it provides[/28]. When I’m trying to master a difficult chord progression, I literally can't think about my mortgage or my deadlines. It’s total immersion. || Speaker 4: I joined a community brass band and started the trumpet. [24]I was looking for a way to meet more people in my neighbourhood after working from home for three years[/24]. I was getting a bit of cabin fever. It’s been wonderful. [29]I’ve gained so much more confidence in public speaking because of it[/29]. If you can play a solo in front of a hundred people, giving a presentation at work suddenly feels like no big deal at all. || Speaker 5: I’m learning the piano because [25]I’d read that it’s one of the best ways to keep the brain plastic and prevent memory loss as you age[/25]. I’m a big believer in lifelong learning. What’s been the most unexpected benefit, though, is [30]the sense of humility it’s taught me[/30]. As a senior manager, I’m used to being the expert, but being a complete beginner again and making constant mistakes has been a very healthy experience for my ego.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker started learning their instrument.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 'regretted giving up' music as a child and had 'unfinished business'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 needed a 'physical way to vent' frustration from their surgical job.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted to play songs for their kids without 'pressure' from a teacher.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to meet people after 'working from home' and feeling isolated.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 read it was a way to 'keep the brain plastic' and prevent memory loss.

options.A: to prove a point to a colleague
options.B: to perform for a family audience
options.C: to prepare for a specific career move
options.D: to compensate for a past mistake
options.E: to help them relax before bed
options.F: to combat a sense of social isolation
options.G: to find an outlet for work-related stress

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has found most rewarding.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 is more aware of 'pitch, tone, and the rhythm' of voices now.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 says it helped their 'coordination' and made them 'dexterous' in surgery.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 values the 'mental escape' and 'total immersion' it provides.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 gained 'confidence in public speaking' through performing solos.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 values the 'sense of humility' and being a beginner again.

options.B: the chance to travel for performances
options.D: the discovery of a hidden talent
options.G: a more grounded sense of self
options.H: the ability to disconnect from daily worries
